title: Good: Early rejection of runt frames with SECURITY comment
description:
lichen_l2.c lines 79-94 define LICHEN_MIN_FRAME_LEN with a detailed SECURITY comment:

```c
/*
 * Minimum valid LICHEN frame size.
 *
 * Wire format (spec section 4, python/src/lichen/link/frame.py):
 *   Length(1) + LLSec(1) + Epoch(1) + SeqNum(2) + DstAddr(0-8) + Payload + MIC(4-8)
 *
 * Absolute minimum (broadcast, 32-bit MIC, empty payload):
 *   1 + 1 + 1 + 2 + 0 + 0 + 4 = 9 bytes
 *
 * SECURITY: Early rejection of runt frames prevents out-of-bounds reads
 * in lichen_link_rx() before MIC validation can occur.
 */
#define LICHEN_MIN_FRAME_LEN 9
```

The check is performed at lichen_l2.c:659-662 before taking the rx_mutex, preventing denial-of-service via runt frame spam that would otherwise contend for the mutex.

What goes right: The constant derivation is fully documented with reference to the spec. The SECURITY comment explains the defensive purpose. The check happens early in the code path. The BUILD_ASSERT at lines 109-116 validates the derivation.

Worth defending: This is defense-in-depth. Even if lichen_link_rx() also checks, catching bad frames earlier is better.
close_reason: Positive finding - pattern to preserve, not an issue
